message:
Welcome to Memory Lane, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

 *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands*
  *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once you're ready just click start to begin

I just received an alert: *Currency Service Timeout Alert*

Service: Currency Service
Urgency: Critical

Maybe check the recent deployments and see if anything has changed that could be impacting service stability. @PLAYER can you jump on and investigate?
I am the incident commander. I'm in control

I've started looking into it. So far, it seems like customers can't see the prices of items on the website. This is definitely affecting their ability to shop and complete purchases.
I can't see any prices on the website at all

Looking into the sales data, I'm seeing a sharp and sudden drop in sales, which is making me wonder if the price issue is the only cause. I'm still working on confirming that, but it's something to consider as we investigate further.
We are getting complaints from all regions. The issue is widespread.
*Incident Number INC100016:* P1 Customers unable to view price of items on the website
Here is a quick summary of the reports so far:

let me look into the logs

https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ULPUBLIC/pages/1114669059/Memory+Lane-+Change+Request+Logs

:star: Reviewed changes

In the meantime, it might be worth considering a server restart. If the logs are being blocked due to a temporary service issue, a restart could help

I'm starting to see some errors in the logs.
Currency service has crashed again! Just checked the site prices are gone again. Back to square one

```
server kernel: No space left on device while writing logs for process nginx 
server nginx[3241]: [emerg] open() /var/log/nginx/currency.logAudit failed (28: No space left on device)
```
@tanya, can you share the analysis with @daniel and @shay please? It is better to have multiple eyes on the problem
Looks like we're dealing with a disk storage issue.
Might be worth swarming on this to figure out what's going on.
@tanya please can you take a look at the disk storage usage for that service
Yep lets get all eyes on it, @hamed , please can you assist as well
:star: Escalating when required
Hey sorry, can I get caught up on what's happening here?
The disk seems to be full on the currency service. @tanya is investigating what is going on, but we might need to scale the disk
@hamed the disk seems to be full on the currency service. @Tanya is investigating what is going on, but we might need to scale the disk
Can you check , the prices seems to be down for me
Ohh okay, I'm caught up. I think I've got some more info that might help.
Root partition is full (100%). `/var/log/nginx/currency.logAudit` alone is over 45GB.
The logs aren't archiving need to figure out what's causing that.
Okay, can we scale the disk up in the mean time?
I did check that at the beginning - we are max'ed our capacity
@hamed , alternatively we could delete some of the log audit files to unblock the issue?
Yep, the issue has re-occurred. We think we have a root cause, we are now planning mitigation steps ASAP
Dont tell me that
It contaisn some historical prices data
Ok, I'm going to compare the configuration of the old and new infrastructure.
@hamed is that data backed up somewhere else? It is currently causing instability for live customers
Checking
Ohh, I see the cause of the issue. Logrotate is missing in the new infrastructure.
That's why the log files aren't being archived and the disk is full.
Sorry @Tanya, what does Logrotate do?
i think th eold servers are already Decomm'ed
Okay, @tanya can we enable that now?

Logrotate automatically rotates, compresses, removes, and manages log files based on predefined rules to save disk space and maintain system logs efficiently. I missed this as part of the migration to the new infra.
So the log files aren't being archived, and the disk is full.
I used ` logrotate --version ` to check, and I'm getting ` bash: logrotate: command not found. `
@Hamed, do we have any workaround for this? Might take a while to get Logrotate installed.
Can you give me what are we working towards?
Any ETA please
Since Logrotate isn't running, we need to compress the log to preserve it and free up space.` Run: gzip /var/log/nginx/currency.logAudit ` from within the service host.
After that, transfer the compressed file to your machine using your usual tooling, then upload it to our long-term storage for retention."
That sounds good, please action @tanya
Hey, just flagging these logs may contain sensitive user data or internal system info, so we need to handle them with care.
Store the downloaded log only on an encrypted, company-managed device. Upload it to our long-term storage, and link it in the incident report.

Hameed has a suggestion which we are implementing now
I'll give you another update in 3 mins
:star: Track actions and time box them
Not sure why the logs aren't archiving, but since we're using disk storage on the server, and the file growth is filling it up.
Ok @PLAYER, happy to proceed with this plan?
As a result, the host can't write new data, which caused a backlog of requests that can't be processed hence this incident.
@Tanya, any theories on why the disk is full?
We could delete the log files and restart the service, that should restore the system and make users happy.
I saw you mentioned we moved to new infra for the Currency Service do you think it could be related? Also open to suggestions on how to fix this.
Hey complaints from users are climbing. They're really not happy can we resolve soon?
@tanya yeah please proceed with gzip plan

Okay

:star: Attempt to replicate the issue

@bez we have resolved the issue and the site is back up
@bob please can you update the customers to tell them that the incident is resolved
Perfect, thanks a ton
i will
@hamed, how urgent is it for us to get a log rotate script in? It looks like we have now got >40GB of storage available
The disk might get filled pretty soon , @Tanya
I'm working on getting the log rotation automation back in place. We'll also monitor the system to ensure long-term stability.

```
/etc/logrotate.d/currency-service:

/var/log/nginx/currency.logAudit {
    daily
    rotate 7
    compress
    missingok
    notifempty
    create 0640 root adm
    postrotate
        [ -s /run/nginx.pid ] && kill -USR1 $(cat /run/nginx.pid)
        /usr/local/bin/upload-to-storage /var/log/nginx/currency.logAudit.1.gz
    endscript
}
```
Ok I've added the log rotation configuration to the new infrastructure. We should not see this issue again.
@hamed , please review the above script to confirm it is what you'd expect
We will push it to prod tonight
@hamed sounds good, have we got enough capacity to get us through to that?
Yes, we have- took 2 weeks to get full
Okay thanks @hamed , I think that means the incident is resolved.
yes
The system is stable now, and we've resolved the immediate issue with disk space. Let's make sure we reintroduce the automated log rotation to prevent this from happening again.
Agreed. We need to reintroduce the log rotation automation and ensure it works properly to prevent manual interventions like this in the future.

To clarify the technical side, here's what happened: The disk space issue was caused by the ` currency.logAudit ` file growing too large. Since we're using disk storage on the server, the file growth filled it up. As a result, the service couldn't write new data, which caused a backlog of requests that couldn't be processed.

Eventually, the system couldn't keep up with the backlog of requests caused by the full disk. This is why users couldn't see prices or complete checkouts. The log rotation script would have kept the currency.logAudit file in check, but it was missed during the infrastructure migration.
I'll work on getting the log rotation automation back in place. We'll also monitor the system to ensure long-term stability.
I'll continue monitoring and will make sure we implement the proper automation to keep everything running smoothly going forward.
